---
title: "meta-pipe: An LLM-agent pipeline for end-to-end automated systematic review and meta-analysis"
author:
  - name: "Hsieh-Ting Lin"
    affiliations:
      - name: "Institution"
        department: "Department"
    orcid: "0000-0000-0000-0000"
    corresponding: true
    email: "author@example.com"
date: last-modified
abstract: |
  **Background**: Systematic reviews and meta-analyses (SR/MA) are the gold standard for
  clinical evidence synthesis but require an estimated 100+ person-hours and 12-18 months
  to complete. Existing automation tools such as ASReview, Rayyan, and TrialMind address
  only 1-3 of the 10+ stages in the SR/MA workflow, leaving statistical analysis and
  manuscript generation entirely manual.

  **Methods**: We developed meta-pipe, a modular LLM-agent pipeline that orchestrates the
  complete SR/MA workflow from topic brainstorming (Stage 00) through submission-ready
  manuscript (Stage 10). The pipeline integrates Claude AI for screening and data extraction,
  Python for automation (24 scripts, ~3,600 lines of code), R (meta/metafor) for statistical
  analysis, and Quarto for manuscript rendering. Quality gates enforce PRISMA compliance,
  dual-review agreement (kappa >=0.60), GRADE assessment, and automated overclaim detection.

  **Results**: We demonstrate meta-pipe on a meta-analysis of immune checkpoint inhibitors
  in triple-negative breast cancer (5 RCTs, N=2,402). The pipeline produced a publication-ready
  manuscript in approximately 14 hours, reporting pathologic complete response RR 1.26
  (95% CI 1.16-1.37, p=0.0015, I^2^=0%, GRADE HIGH). Publication readiness score reached
  95-98%, with PRISMA compliance at 27/27 items.

  **Conclusions**: meta-pipe is the first end-to-end AI-assisted pipeline covering all stages
  of systematic review and meta-analysis. Its human-in-the-loop design with automated quality
  gates addresses concerns about standalone AI use in evidence synthesis while achieving
  75-80% time savings compared with manual workflows.
keywords:
  - systematic review
  - meta-analysis
  - large language model
  - automation
  - evidence synthesis
  - artificial intelligence
bibliography: references.bib
csl: style.csl
---

{{< include 00_abstract.qmd >}}

{{< pagebreak >}}

{{< include 01_introduction.qmd >}}

{{< pagebreak >}}

{{< include 02_methods.qmd >}}

{{< pagebreak >}}

{{< include 03_results.qmd >}}

{{< pagebreak >}}

{{< include 04_discussion.qmd >}}

{{< include tables.qmd >}}

{{< include figures_legends.qmd >}}

# References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::
